user.title: Connect to Asmoke Cloud
user.description:
Before continuing, note that the MQTT username and password are intentionally not stored in the public repository. If those values are not already prefilled locally, contact the maintainer directly for setup help instead of searching the repo. Local auth status: {local_auth}

Auto discovery only learns the device ID after Home Assistant can log in to the broker. For the best chance of discovery, turn on the smoker and open the Asmoke app on a phone connected to the same local network as the smoker so the device publishes a fresh status message. Click Next to choose a setup method.

setup_method.title: Choose setup method
setup_method.description: Choose auto discovery if you already have working broker credentials and want Home Assistant to learn the device ID. Choose manual setup if you already know the device ID.

discover.title: Discover Asmoke device
discover.description:
Home Assistant will temporarily listen for Asmoke messages for about 45 seconds to find candidate device IDs after logging in to the MQTT broker. This step still requires working broker credentials, and those credentials are intentionally not published in the repository. If you do not already have them in a local auth file, contact the maintainer directly for setup help.

Before continuing, turn on the smoker and open the Asmoke app on a phone connected to the same local network as the smoker so the device publishes fresh messages. If local auth defaults are present, the broker fields are prefilled automatically. Local auth status: {local_auth}

confirm_discovery.title: Confirm discovered Asmoke device

manual.title: Enter device ID manually
manual.description: Enter your Asmoke device ID together with working MQTT broker credentials. The broker username and password are intentionally not published in the repository. If you do not already have them in a local auth file, contact the maintainer directly for setup help. If local auth defaults are present, the fields are prefilled automatically. Local auth status: {local_auth}

reauth_confirm.title: Update broker credentials
reauth_confirm.description: Broker authentication failed. Update the broker settings to continue.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Asmoke MQTT broker
error.device_not_found: No Asmoke device message was received during discovery
error.invalid_auth: Broker authentication failed. The public repository does not include working MQTT credentials.
error.unknown_device: Choose one of the discovered Asmoke devices

abort.already_configured: This Asmoke device is already configured

entry_id.description: Optional config entry ID if multiple Asmoke integrations are loaded.

target_temp.description: Target grill temperature to publish with the Smoke command.

start_cook.name: Start cook

entry_id.name: Config entry ID
entry_id.description: Optional config entry ID if multiple Asmoke integrations are loaded.

device_id.name: Asmoke device ID
device_id.description: Optional Asmoke device ID if multiple entries are loaded.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: Confirmed mode to start, for example smoke, quick, or roast.

target_temp.name: Target temperature
target_temp.description: Target grill temperature to publish with the selected cook command.

target_time.name: Target time
target_time.description: Target cook time in minutes for confirmed modes that require a duration.

probe_temp.name: Probe temperature
probe_temp.description: Target probe temperature for the confirmed Roast command.

ingredient_category.name: Ingredient category
ingredient_category.description: Confirmed ingredient category value for the Roast command, for example Beef.

k_value.name: K value
k_value.description: Confirmed kValue string for the Roast command.

entry_id.description: Optional config entry ID if multiple Asmoke integrations are loaded.
